articles.headline: Supreme Court Defines Scope of 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Digital Privacy Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment clarifying the 'Right to be Forgotten' (RTBF) in India's digital landscape.
• The ruling provides guidelines for individuals seeking to remove personal data from public digital platforms under specific conditions.
• It emphasizes a delicate balance between an individual's right to privacy and the public's right to information, especially concerning historical public records and matters of public interest.
• The court directed the government to consider comprehensive legislative frameworks for data erasure and digital privacy in the evolving information age.
• This decision is expected to significantly impact data protection laws, online content regulation, and the future of digital rights in India.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Right to be Forgotten is an evolving concept, often linked to Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty).
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its provisions related to data principal rights.
Landmark judgments related to privacy, such as the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
Concept of data fiduciaries and data principals under Indian data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges of balancing the Right to be Forgotten with freedom of expression (Article 19) and the public's right to information.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in evolving digital rights and adapting constitutional principles to new technological realities.
Examine the implications of this ruling for data governance, content regulation, and individual autonomy in the digital sphere.
Suggest legislative measures and policy reforms required for effective implementation and enforcement of RTBF in India.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred image of digital data streams with a prominent gavel and legal documents, symbolizing digital justice.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Right to be Forgotten' gained international prominence with the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in 2016.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and its dimensions

articles.headline: Ranveer Allahbadia Controversy: Nation Debates Free Speech vs. Obscene Content on Social Media
articles.explainer:
• Remarks made by Ranveer Allahbadia on the show 'India's Got Latent' have sparked nationwide outrage and intense debate.
• The controversy primarily revolves around the limits of free speech, particularly concerning obscene or inappropriate content on digital platforms.
• Legal experts and civil society groups are discussing the applicability and enforcement of laws pertaining to obscenity and content moderation on social media.
• The incident has reignited calls for clearer guidelines and stricter enforcement mechanisms for user-generated content.
• It highlights the ongoing challenge of balancing creative expression with societal norms and legal frameworks in the digital age.
prelimsPoints: Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
Article 19(2) outlines reasonable restrictions on free speech, including public order, decency, and morality.
Sections of the Information Technology Act, 2000, dealing with obscene content and intermediary liability.
Definition of 'obscenity' as interpreted by Indian courts (e.g., Ranjit Udeshi case).
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in defining and regulating obscene content in the context of evolving social media platforms and diverse user bases.
Critically analyze the current legal framework in India for content moderation and its effectiveness in balancing free speech with public decency.
Examine the role of social media intermediaries in controlling objectionable content and their responsibilities under Indian law.
Suggest policy recommendations for fostering responsible digital citizenship and creating a safer online environment while upholding fundamental rights.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized illustration of a speaker icon with blurred social media feeds in the background, representing online discourse.
articles.trivia: The 'safe harbour' provisions under Section 79 of the IT Act protect intermediaries from liability for third-party content under certain conditions.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Freedom of Speech and Expression and its reasonable restrictions

articles.headline: Sarod Maestro Gurdev Singh Passes Away at 77, Leaves Rich Musical Legacy
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Sarod player Gurdev Singh, a towering figure in Indian classical music, passed away at the age of 77.
• Singh was celebrated for his unique rendition style and significant contributions to popularizing the sarod globally.
• A recipient of numerous national awards, he mentored several generations of musicians, enriching the Maihar Gharana tradition.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Indian classical music, prompting tributes from artists and cultural institutions nationwide.
• Gurdev Singh's legacy includes extensive recordings and a profound impact on the evolution of Hindustani classical music.
gsSubTopics: Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: The Sarod is a stringed instrument, prominent in Hindustani classical music.
Maihar Gharana is a prominent school of Hindustani classical music, known for its instrumental tradition.
Other famous Sarod players (e.g., Amjad Ali Khan, Ali Akbar Khan).
Major awards in Indian classical music (e.g., Sangeet Natak Akademi Award, Padma awards).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Guru-Shishya parampara in preserving and propagating Indian classical music traditions.
Analyze the evolution and contribution of the Sarod as a prominent instrument in Hindustani classical music.
Examine the role of cultural institutions and government initiatives in promoting and sustaining traditional Indian art forms.
Evaluate the challenges faced by classical musicians in the contemporary era and potential solutions for their sustenance.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Gurdev Singh playing the Sarod, capturing his intense musical focus.
articles.trivia: The Sarod is believed to have evolved from the Afghan rubab and has no frets, allowing for continuous glides between notes (meend).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Art and Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Classical Music and Musical Instruments

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation and Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects a cautious approach amidst global uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures, despite a positive growth outlook.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to remain vigilant on inflation, targeting the 4% medium-term goal.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Experts anticipate continued stability in lending rates, supporting economic recovery while managing price stability concerns.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Key objectives of monetary policy in India: price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government for the RBI (currently 4% with a band of +/- 2%).
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst prevailing economic conditions, both domestic and global.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the imperative of supporting economic recovery.
Examine the implications of the MPC's stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting an upward trending arrow indicating growth, superimposed with the RBI logo and financial charts.
articles.trivia: The first meeting of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was held in October 2016.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Leaders Pledge Enhanced Cooperation in Maritime Security and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key outcomes included pledges for deeper cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and disaster management in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both sides emphasized boosting bilateral trade and investment, with a focus on supply chain resilience and digital economy integration.
• Discussions also covered regional connectivity projects and strengthening people-to-people ties.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic alignment with the ASEAN bloc.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) members and its headquarters.
India's 'Act East Policy' (formerly 'Look East Policy') objectives.
Importance of the Indo-Pacific region for global trade and security.
Major initiatives under India-ASEAN cooperation (e.g., Delhi Dialogue, India-ASEAN Free Trade Area).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic imperatives driving India's enhanced engagement with ASEAN in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the significance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN for regional stability and freedom of navigation.
Evaluate the potential for boosting trade and economic integration between India and the ASEAN bloc, identifying key opportunities and challenges.
Examine how India's 'Act East Policy' contributes to its broader foreign policy objectives and its role as a regional power.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states waving together, symbolizing partnership and cooperation.
articles.trivia: India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1995, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: ASEAN and India's relations with it

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a crucial step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
• This technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making India a more competitive player in the global space market.
• The RLV program is part of ISRO's long-term vision to enhance space access and capabilities.
• Scientists lauded the precision of the autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems during the complex maneuver.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program.
Concept of reusable launch vehicles and their economic advantages.
Difference between expendable and reusable launch vehicles.
Other countries/agencies developing RLV technology (e.g., SpaceX, Blue Origin).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of reusable launch vehicles compared to traditional expendable rockets.
Examine the technological challenges involved in designing and operating RLVs, including materials science, guidance systems, and thermal protection.
Evaluate how ISRO's RLV program aligns with India's broader ambitions in space exploration and commercial space services.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a controlled landing.
articles.trivia: ISRO first conducted a successful RLV-TD atmospheric re-entry experiment in 2016.

articles.headline: Government Launches Phase 2 of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Boosts Incentives
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and demand.
• This phase introduces enhanced financial incentives for both electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key objectives include reducing fossil fuel imports, creating new job opportunities, and promoting indigenous manufacturing capabilities.
• Stakeholders praised the government's commitment to accelerating the transition to a sustainable energy future.
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme
prelimsPoints: Definition of 'Green Hydrogen' (produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy).
Objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Difference between green, grey, and blue hydrogen.
Role of electrolysers in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security, climate change commitments, and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentives and policy frameworks in promoting indigenous manufacturing and demand for green hydrogen.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen on various sectors of the Indian economy and its contribution to decarbonization efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a leaf merging with a hydrogen atom symbol, representing green hydrogen.
articles.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

articles.headline: Parliament Approves 'Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2025' for Decriminalizing Minor Offences
articles.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the 'Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2025', a landmark legislation aimed at decriminalizing minor economic and procedural offences.
• The Bill proposes to amend various acts, replacing imprisonment with monetary penalties for minor violations, thereby reducing the burden on the judicial system.
• It seeks to promote ease of doing business and living by fostering trust-based governance and reducing legal complexities for citizens and businesses.
• The legislation is expected to significantly decongest courts and improve the efficiency of the justice delivery system.
• Industry bodies and legal experts have welcomed the move as a progressive step towards modernizing India's regulatory framework.
prelimsPoints: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2023 (original bill).
Concept of decriminalization and its benefits.
Impact of minor offences on the judicial system and ease of doing business.
Role of various ministries in identifying and amending laws for decriminalization.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2025 promotes 'ease of doing business' and 'ease of living' in India.
Analyze the potential impact of decriminalizing minor offences on the Indian judicial system and its efficiency.
Critically evaluate the balance struck by the Bill between deterrence and trust-based governance.
Examine the broader implications of such reforms for India's regulatory environment and its attractiveness for investment.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a balance scale with 'Justice' on one side and 'Ease of Business' on the other, symbolizing the bill's objectives.
articles.trivia: The original Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2023, proposed to amend 183 provisions across 42 Central Acts.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Ease of Doing Business and Judicial Reforms

articles.headline: India's Industrial Production Records Robust Growth in Q3, Manufacturing Sector Leads Recovery
articles.explainer:
• India's industrial production registered robust growth in the third quarter of the fiscal year 2024-25, exceeding market expectations.
• The manufacturing sector emerged as the primary driver of this recovery, showing significant expansion across various sub-sectors.
• Data from the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation indicated positive trends in capital goods and consumer durables segments.
• This growth is attributed to strong domestic demand, government infrastructure spending, and improving global supply chains.
• Economists view this as a positive sign for overall economic recovery and job creation in the coming months.
prelimsPoints: Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is a composite indicator that measures the growth rate in different industry groups of the economy.
IIP is compiled and published by the Central Statistical Office (CSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
Eight core industries that have a significant impact on IIP.
Components of IIP: Manufacturing, Mining, and Electricity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth in India's industrial production, particularly the manufacturing sector, in the recent quarter.
Discuss the significance of industrial growth for overall economic recovery, job creation, and achieving India's development goals.
Examine the role of government policies, such as 'Make in India' and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes, in boosting manufacturing output.
Evaluate the challenges that persist for the industrial sector, including global trade uncertainties and supply chain disruptions, and suggest measures to sustain growth.
articles.imageDescription: A factory floor with machinery in operation, symbolizing industrial growth and productivity.
articles.trivia: The base year for the Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is currently 2011-12.

articles.headline: UN Human Rights Council Discusses Global Refugee Crisis, India Reiterates Humanitarian Aid
articles.explainer:
• The United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC) convened to address the escalating global refugee crisis and discuss international response strategies.
• Member states highlighted the urgent need for increased humanitarian aid, protection for displaced populations, and durable solutions for refugees.
• India reiterated its long-standing commitment to humanitarian principles, emphasizing its history of providing assistance to refugees and displaced persons.
• The discussions focused on shared responsibility, equitable burden-sharing, and addressing root causes of forced displacement.
• The UNHRC called for greater international cooperation and adherence to international refugee law.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: UN Human Rights Council (UNHRC) structure, mandate, and headquarters.
Difference between a refugee, an asylum seeker, and an internally displaced person.
India's stance on the 1951 Refugee Convention and its 1967 Protocol (India is not a signatory).
Key international organizations dealing with refugees (e.g., UNHCR).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical approach and humanitarian principles in dealing with refugee populations, despite not being a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention.
Analyze the challenges posed by the global refugee crisis for international peace, security, and human rights.
Critically evaluate the concept of 'shared responsibility' and 'burden-sharing' in addressing forced migration on a global scale.
Suggest policy measures for India to develop a comprehensive and consistent national refugee policy in line with international best practices and its humanitarian ethos.
articles.imageDescription: A symbolic image of a group of people walking towards a distant horizon, representing displaced populations.
articles.trivia: The principle of 'non-refoulement' prohibits countries from returning refugees to places where their lives or freedom would be threatened.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review: Focus on Vocational Training Integration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights significant progress in integrating vocational education into mainstream schooling from an early age.
• Special emphasis was placed on skill development, industry linkages, and experiential learning across educational levels.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to quality vocational programs.
• The review underscores the government's commitment to making education holistic, flexible, and employment-oriented.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of the National Education Policy 2020.
Recommendations of NEP 2020 regarding vocational education integration.
Role of various bodies like National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC).
Concept of 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrating vocational training into mainstream education as envisioned by the National Education Policy 2020 for India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the challenges faced in the effective implementation of vocational education reforms under NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure and teacher training.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 aims to make education more holistic, flexible, and employment-oriented, and its potential impact on India's workforce.
Suggest strategies to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality vocational education across all regions and socio-economic strata.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in a vocational training workshop, symbolizing practical skill development.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 aims to achieve 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2035.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: National Education Policy and its impact

articles.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Along LAC Picks Up Pace Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly accelerated the development of critical border infrastructure along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.
• The focus is on improving road networks, bridges, tunnels, and forward airbases to enhance military mobility and logistical capabilities.
• This intensified development is part of India's strategic response to ongoing border tensions and aims to bolster national security.
• The projects are being executed by various agencies, including the Border Roads Organisation (BRO), employing advanced construction techniques.
• Experts view this as a crucial step to bridge the infrastructure gap with adversaries and ensure effective troop deployment and rapid response.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Line of Actual Control (LAC) vs. Line of Control (LoC).
Role and mandate of Border Roads Organisation (BRO).
Strategic importance of infrastructure development in border areas.
Key passes and geographical features along the LAC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic rationale behind India's accelerated border infrastructure development along the LAC in the context of persistent border tensions with China.
Discuss the impact of improved infrastructure on India's military preparedness, logistical capabilities, and overall national security in the Himalayan region.
Evaluate the challenges faced in executing large-scale infrastructure projects in difficult terrain and adverse weather conditions.
Examine how this infrastructure push contributes to India's broader geopolitical strategy and its efforts to assert its sovereignty in disputed territories.
articles.imageDescription: Heavy machinery constructing a road in a mountainous, snowy border region, symbolizing infrastructure development.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was formed in 1960 and constructs and maintains road networks in India's border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

articles.headline: AI in Healthcare: Government Launches Initiative for Early Disease Detection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launched a new national initiative leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI) for early disease detection.
• The program focuses on developing AI-powered diagnostic tools for prevalent diseases like tuberculosis, diabetes, and certain cancers, especially in rural areas.
• It aims to enhance diagnostic accuracy, reduce healthcare costs, and improve access to timely medical interventions.
• The initiative will involve public-private partnerships, data anonymization, and ethical AI development guidelines.
• This marks a significant step towards modernizing India's healthcare system through technological innovation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Basic concepts of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
Applications of AI in various sectors, particularly healthcare.
Government initiatives for digital health in India (e.g., Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission).
Ethical considerations in AI development and deployment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing public healthcare in India, particularly in early disease detection and diagnosis.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with integrating AI into India's diverse healthcare ecosystem, including data availability, infrastructure, and skilled manpower.
Evaluate the ethical considerations and regulatory frameworks required to ensure responsible and equitable deployment of AI in healthcare.
Examine how such initiatives can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving health outcomes in remote and underserved areas.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor examining an X-ray image with AI-generated overlays highlighting potential disease areas.
articles.trivia: AI algorithms can analyze medical images, patient data, and genomic information to identify patterns indicative of diseases, often with greater speed and accuracy than humans.

articles.headline: Wetland Conservation Efforts Intensified Ahead of World Wetlands Day
articles.explainer:
• India has stepped up its wetland conservation and management efforts across the country, aligning with global commitments ahead of World Wetlands Day (February 2).
• New initiatives focus on restoring degraded wetlands, preventing encroachment, and promoting sustainable utilization of wetland resources.
• Community participation and awareness campaigns are being emphasized to ensure long-term ecological health of these vital ecosystems.
• Wetlands play a crucial role in biodiversity conservation, flood control, water purification, and supporting livelihoods.
• The government reiterates its commitment to the Ramsar Convention and expanding the network of Ramsar Sites in India.
prelimsPoints: World Wetlands Day is celebrated annually on February 2nd.
Ramsar Convention (1971) on Wetlands of International Importance, especially as Waterfowl Habitat.
Key characteristics and ecological importance of wetlands.
Examples of major Ramsar Sites in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the various ecosystem services they provide, including their role in climate change mitigation and adaptation.
Analyze the major threats to wetlands in India, such as pollution, encroachment, and unsustainable development, and their environmental consequences.
Evaluate the effectiveness of India's conservation policies and programs, including the National Plan for Conservation of Aquatic Ecosystems (NPCA) and the Ramsar Convention.
Suggest integrated approaches for sustainable management of wetlands, emphasizing community involvement, inter-sectoral coordination, and scientific research.
articles.imageDescription: A serene landscape of a wetland with migratory birds, reflecting its ecological beauty and importance.
articles.trivia: The Ramsar Convention is the only global treaty dedicated to a single ecosystem type.

articles.headline: Restoration Work Begins on Ancient Buddhist Sites in Bihar, Boosts Heritage Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated major restoration and conservation work at several ancient Buddhist sites in Bihar.
• The projects aim to preserve the historical and cultural integrity of these sites, which are crucial to India's Buddhist heritage.
• This initiative is expected to boost cultural tourism, particularly among Buddhist pilgrims from across the globe, and generate local employment.
• Advanced archaeological techniques and collaboration with international heritage bodies are being employed.
• The restoration efforts underscore India's commitment to safeguarding its rich historical legacy and promoting cultural diplomacy.
gsSubTopics: Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: Key Buddhist sites in Bihar (e.g., Bodh Gaya, Nalanda, Rajgir, Vaishali).
Role and functions of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
Major Buddhist architectural styles (e.g., Stupas, Viharas, Chaityas).
Government initiatives for promoting Buddhist tourism (e.g., Buddhist Circuit).
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical and cultural significance of Buddhist sites in Bihar and their importance for India's national heritage and international relations.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and restoration of ancient archaeological sites, including funding, technical expertise, and community engagement.
Evaluate the potential of heritage tourism, particularly Buddhist tourism, to contribute to local economic development and cultural exchange.
Examine the role of government agencies and international collaborations in preserving and promoting India's rich archaeological heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists working carefully at an ancient Buddhist stupa in Bihar, with scaffolding visible.
articles.trivia: Bodh Gaya, where Buddha attained enlightenment, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

articles.headline: E-Governance Push: New Portal Launched for Seamless Delivery of Public Services
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a comprehensive e-governance portal for streamlined public service delivery.
• The platform integrates various government services, enabling citizens to access them digitally from a single window, reducing red tape and improving efficiency.
• It aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen-centric governance through digital transformation.
• Features include online applications, status tracking, digital document submission, and a unified grievance redressal mechanism.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards realizing the vision of 'Digital India' and improving citizen-government interaction.
gsSubTopics: Applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of the Digital India program.
Various e-governance initiatives in India (e.g., MyGov, DigiLocker, UMANG app).
Concept of 'Good Governance' and its components.
Role of Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of e-governance initiatives in promoting transparency, accountability, and citizen-centric service delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits and challenges associated with implementing a single-window e-governance portal for accessing diverse public services.
Evaluate how digital transformation in governance contributes to 'ease of living' and reduces corruption and red tape.
Suggest measures to ensure digital inclusion, address privacy concerns, and enhance the cybersecurity of such e-governance platforms.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized hand interacting with a holographic interface displaying various government service icons.
articles.trivia: The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app provides access to over 1200 government services on mobile.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting, Focus on Global Economic Stability
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, focusing on global economic stability, sustainable finance, and debt vulnerabilities.
• Discussions addressed macroeconomic challenges, including inflation, supply chain disruptions, and climate finance for developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for coordinated global policy responses to foster inclusive and resilient economic growth.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on strengthening multilateral development banks and reforming international financial architecture.
• Delegates underscored the importance of digital public infrastructure and financial inclusion for global economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
prelimsPoints: G20 members and its formation.
Objectives of the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
Concept of climate finance and its importance for developing countries.
Role of multilateral development banks (e.g., World Bank, IMF).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and priorities in steering the G20 agenda, particularly concerning global economic stability, sustainable finance, and debt relief for vulnerable nations.
Analyze the challenges faced by the global economy, such as persistent inflation, geopolitical fragmentation, and climate change, and the need for coordinated international responses.
Evaluate the significance of reforming multilateral development banks and strengthening the international financial architecture for equitable global growth.
Examine the potential of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and financial inclusion in driving sustainable economic development and bridging global inequalities.
articles.imageDescription: A conference table with flags of G20 nations, symbolizing international cooperation and economic discussions.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

articles.headline: National Mission on Interlinking Rivers: Progress on Key Projects Reviewed
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti reviewed the progress of several priority projects under the National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers (ILR).
• The review focused on the Ken-Betwa Link Project and other proposed links aimed at addressing water scarcity and flood management.
• Environmental clearances, land acquisition, and financial viability remain key challenges for these large-scale infrastructure projects.
• The mission seeks to transfer water from surplus basins to deficit basins, ensuring equitable water distribution and agricultural prosperity.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for integrated water resource management and addressing ecological concerns.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure (Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.)
prelimsPoints: Concept and objectives of the National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers.
The Ken-Betwa Link Project: involved rivers, states, and its significance.
Arguments for and against river interlinking projects (environmental, social, economic).
Role of the National Water Development Agency (NWDA).
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the feasibility and desirability of the National Mission on Interlinking Rivers in addressing India's water challenges, considering both its potential benefits and associated risks.
Analyze the environmental, social, and economic implications of large-scale river interlinking projects, with specific reference to the Ken-Betwa Link Project.
Discuss the challenges in obtaining environmental clearances and ensuring sustainable development practices for such mega-projects.
Suggest alternative or complementary strategies for integrated water resource management that can mitigate water scarcity and manage floods effectively.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of two rivers being connected by a canal through a landscape, symbolizing river interlinking.
articles.trivia: The idea of interlinking Indian rivers dates back to the 19th century, with a proposal by British engineer Sir Arthur Cotton.

articles.headline: India's First Fully Private Sector Defence Manufacturing Plant Inaugurated
articles.explainer:
• India's first fully private sector defence manufacturing plant was inaugurated, marking a significant milestone in the 'Make in India' defence initiative.
• The facility will specialize in producing advanced artillery systems and precision munitions, enhancing indigenous capabilities.
• This move aims to reduce India's reliance on defence imports and boost self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in the strategic sector.
• The plant is expected to create numerous job opportunities and foster a robust defence industrial ecosystem.
• Defence analysts view this as a pivotal step towards India emerging as a global defence manufacturing hub.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Government policies to promote private sector participation in defence manufacturing.
Objectives of the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in the defence sector.
Different categories of defence procurement (Buy (Indian), Buy and Make (Indian), etc.).
Role of Defence Public Sector Undertakings (DPSUs) vs. private players.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of increased private sector participation in India's defence manufacturing for achieving self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) and enhancing national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for private defence manufacturers in India, including access to technology, funding, and export markets.
Evaluate the impact of such initiatives on India's defence industrial base, job creation, and technological advancement.
Examine how government policies and reforms are creating a conducive environment for the private sector to contribute to defence production.
articles.imageDescription: A modern defence manufacturing facility with advanced machinery and technicians at work.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, making indigenous manufacturing a strategic imperative.

articles.headline: New Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure Sectors Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) unveiled a new comprehensive cybersecurity framework.
• The framework is designed to protect India's critical infrastructure sectors, including energy, finance, transport, and digital services, from sophisticated cyber threats.
• It mandates stricter compliance standards, regular audits, and enhanced threat intelligence sharing among stakeholders.
• The move comes amidst a rise in cyberattacks globally, highlighting the vulnerability of essential services to digital disruptions.
• Experts emphasize the need for robust public-private cooperation and skilled cybersecurity workforce development to effectively implement the framework.
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: Definition of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Role and mandate of National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC).
Role of Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In).
Key types of cyberattacks (e.g., ransomware, phishing, DDoS).
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing importance of cybersecurity for national security and economic stability, particularly concerning critical infrastructure sectors.
Analyze the key features and objectives of the new cybersecurity framework for critical infrastructure and its potential impact on mitigating cyber threats.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework across diverse critical sectors, including technological gaps, human resources, and inter-agency coordination.
Suggest measures to strengthen India's cybersecurity posture, emphasizing public-private partnerships, international cooperation, and skill development.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed over a network of interconnected critical infrastructure symbols.
articles.trivia: The NCIIPC was established under Section 70A of the Information Technology Act, 2000.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to internal security through communication networks

highlights: Supreme Court delivers landmark ruling on 'Right to be Forgotten', balancing privacy with public interest in the digital age.
Ranveer Allahbadia controversy sparks national debate on free speech, obscenity, and social media content regulation.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, signaling cautious optimism on inflation and economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
ISRO successfully tests next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle technology demonstrator, aiming for cost-effective space access.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes with pledges for enhanced cooperation in maritime security, trade, and regional connectivity.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Right to be Forgotten' (RTBF) in India, consider the following statements:
1. It is explicitly enshrined as a fundamental right under Article 19 of the Indian Constitution.
2. The Supreme Court of India has recently provided guidelines for its applicability, balancing individual privacy with the public's right to information.
3. The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, is the primary legislation that fully operationalizes the RTBF in India.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Right to be Forgotten is not explicitly enshrined in Article 19; it is an evolving concept linked to Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) and data protection principles. Statement 2 is correct as per the recent Supreme Court ruling mentioned in the news. Statement 3 is incorrect. While the DPDP Act, 2023, includes provisions for the 'right to erasure' and 'right to correction and completion' of personal data, the full operationalization and judicial interpretation of RTBF are still evolving, and the recent SC ruling clarifies its scope.

prelimsMocks.question: Gurdev Singh, who recently passed away, was a renowned exponent of which of the following musical instruments?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Gurdev Singh was a celebrated Sarod player, a prominent figure in Indian classical music, known for his contributions to the Maihar Gharana.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements best describes the 'Repo Rate' in the context of India's monetary policy?
(a) It is the rate at which commercial banks lend money to the Reserve Bank of India.
(b) It is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
(c) It is the rate at which commercial banks lend money to their customers.
(d) It is the rate at which the government borrows money from the public.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Repo Rate (Repurchase Rate) is the interest rate at which the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) provides liquidity (loans) to commercial banks against government securities. It is a key tool used by the RBI to control inflation and manage money supply in the economy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator:
1. The primary aim of RLV technology is to reduce the cost of space launches.
2. ISRO has successfully tested a controlled landing experiment simulating return from orbit.
3. The RLV program focuses on developing a fully expendable launch vehicle system.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; RLVs aim to significantly reduce launch costs. Statement 2 is correct, as ISRO recently demonstrated a controlled landing. Statement 3 is incorrect; the RLV program focuses on developing a fully reusable launch vehicle system, not an expendable one.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about Green Hydrogen is/are correct?
1. Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
2. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
3. Grey Hydrogen is considered the cleanest form of hydrogen production.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Green Hydrogen is defined by its production method using renewable energy. Statement 2 is correct; this is a core objective of the National Green Hydrogen Mission. Statement 3 is incorrect; Grey Hydrogen is produced from natural gas using steam methane reforming, which releases carbon emissions, making it not the cleanest form. Green Hydrogen is considered the cleanest.

mainsMocks.question: The recent controversy involving Ranveer Allahbadia has reignited the debate on free speech versus content regulation in the digital age. Discuss the challenges in balancing Article 19(1)(a) with reasonable restrictions, and suggest policy measures to foster responsible digital citizenship.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights (Freedom of Speech and Expression), Government Policies and Interventions, Media and Social Media, Cyber Security.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the controversy and the constitutional provision (Article 19). Explain the concept of reasonable restrictions (Article 19(2)) and the difficulty in applying them to digital content. Discuss challenges like defining obscenity, intermediary liability, global nature of internet, and impact on youth. Suggest policy measures like clear guidelines, digital literacy, self-regulation, and effective grievance redressal mechanisms. Conclude with the importance of a balanced approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent ruling on the 'Right to be Forgotten' marks a significant development in India's digital privacy jurisprudence. Evaluate the implications of this right for individual autonomy and public interest, and analyze the legislative steps required to operationalize it effectively.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights (Right to Privacy), Judiciary, Data Protection, Digital Governance.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the 'Right to be Forgotten' (RTBF) and its link to Article 21 and the Puttaswamy judgment. Explain the balance required between individual privacy (right to control personal data, reputation) and public interest (freedom of expression, historical record, public safety). Discuss challenges in implementation. Analyze the role of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and suggest further legislative clarity, regulatory mechanisms, and judicial interpretations for effective operationalization.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Act East' policy has been instrumental in strengthening its ties with ASEAN. In light of the recent India-ASEAN Summit outcomes, analyze India's strategic imperatives behind enhanced cooperation in maritime security and trade with the ASEAN bloc.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations, Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India, Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's 'Act East' policy and the significance of ASEAN. Discuss strategic imperatives: counterbalancing China's influence, ensuring freedom of navigation in the Indo-Pacific, securing sea lanes of communication (SLOCs), and promoting regional stability. For trade, discuss market access, supply chain diversification, and economic integration. Conclude with the importance of India-ASEAN partnership for a multipolar Indo-Pacific.

quickBytes.text: The Right to be Forgotten (RTBF) is linked to Article 21, the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
Article 19(1)(a) guarantees freedom of speech, subject to reasonable restrictions under Article 19(2).
Gurdev Singh was a maestro of the Sarod, a prominent stringed instrument in Hindustani classical music.
Repo Rate is the interest rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks, a key monetary policy tool.
ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established in 1967 with the Bangkok Declaration.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
Green Hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity generated from renewable energy sources.
The Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill aims to decriminalize minor offences to promote ease of doing business.
NEP 2020 promotes vocational training integration from an early age for holistic education.
Wetlands are vital ecosystems, protected globally by the Ramsar Convention, signed in 1971.

GS1.headline: Sarod Maestro Gurdev Singh Passes Away at 77, Leaves Rich Musical Legacy
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Sarod player Gurdev Singh, a towering figure in Indian classical music, passed away at the age of 77.
• Singh was celebrated for his unique rendition style and significant contributions to popularizing the sarod globally.
• A recipient of numerous national awards, he mentored several generations of musicians, enriching the Maihar Gharana tradition.
• His demise marks the end of an era for Indian classical music, prompting tributes from artists and cultural institutions nationwide.
• Gurdev Singh's legacy includes extensive recordings and a profound impact on the evolution of Hindustani classical music.
gsSubTopics: Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: The Sarod is a stringed instrument, prominent in Hindustani classical music.
Maihar Gharana is a prominent school of Hindustani classical music, known for its instrumental tradition.
Other famous Sarod players (e.g., Amjad Ali Khan, Ali Akbar Khan).
Major awards in Indian classical music (e.g., Sangeet Natak Akademi Award, Padma awards).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Guru-Shishya parampara in preserving and propagating Indian classical music traditions.
Analyze the evolution and contribution of the Sarod as a prominent instrument in Hindustani classical music.
Examine the role of cultural institutions and government initiatives in promoting and sustaining traditional Indian art forms.
Evaluate the challenges faced by classical musicians in the contemporary era and potential solutions for their sustenance.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white portrait of Gurdev Singh playing the Sarod, capturing his intense musical focus.
GS1.trivia: The Sarod is believed to have evolved from the Afghan rubab and has no frets, allowing for continuous glides between notes (meend).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Art and Culture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Indian Classical Music and Musical Instruments

GS1.headline: Restoration Work Begins on Ancient Buddhist Sites in Bihar, Boosts Heritage Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated major restoration and conservation work at several ancient Buddhist sites in Bihar.
• The projects aim to preserve the historical and cultural integrity of these sites, which are crucial to India's Buddhist heritage.
• This initiative is expected to boost cultural tourism, particularly among Buddhist pilgrims from across the globe, and generate local employment.
• Advanced archaeological techniques and collaboration with international heritage bodies are being employed.
• The restoration efforts underscore India's commitment to safeguarding its rich historical legacy and promoting cultural diplomacy.
gsSubTopics: Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
prelimsPoints: Key Buddhist sites in Bihar (e.g., Bodh Gaya, Nalanda, Rajgir, Vaishali).
Role and functions of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
Major Buddhist architectural styles (e.g., Stupas, Viharas, Chaityas).
Government initiatives for promoting Buddhist tourism (e.g., Buddhist Circuit).
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical and cultural significance of Buddhist sites in Bihar and their importance for India's national heritage and international relations.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and restoration of ancient archaeological sites, including funding, technical expertise, and community engagement.
Evaluate the potential of heritage tourism, particularly Buddhist tourism, to contribute to local economic development and cultural exchange.
Examine the role of government agencies and international collaborations in preserving and promoting India's rich archaeological heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists working carefully at an ancient Buddhist stupa in Bihar, with scaffolding visible.
GS1.trivia: Bodh Gaya, where Buddha attained enlightenment, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Defines Scope of 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Digital Privacy Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial judgment clarifying the 'Right to be Forgotten' (RTBF) in India's digital landscape.
• The ruling provides guidelines for individuals seeking to remove personal data from public digital platforms under specific conditions.
• It emphasizes a delicate balance between an individual's right to privacy and the public's right to information, especially concerning historical public records and matters of public interest.
• The court directed the government to consider comprehensive legislative frameworks for data erasure and digital privacy in the evolving information age.
• This decision is expected to significantly impact data protection laws, online content regulation, and the future of digital rights in India.
keyTerms: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act)
prelimsPoints: Right to be Forgotten is an evolving concept, often linked to Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty).
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its provisions related to data principal rights.
Landmark judgments related to privacy, such as the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
Concept of data fiduciaries and data principals under Indian data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges of balancing the Right to be Forgotten with freedom of expression (Article 19) and the public's right to information.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in evolving digital rights and adapting constitutional principles to new technological realities.
Examine the implications of this ruling for data governance, content regulation, and individual autonomy in the digital sphere.
Suggest legislative measures and policy reforms required for effective implementation and enforcement of RTBF in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A blurred image of digital data streams with a prominent gavel and legal documents, symbolizing digital justice.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Right to be Forgotten' gained international prominence with the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in 2016.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and its dimensions

GS2.headline: Ranveer Allahbadia Controversy: Nation Debates Free Speech vs. Obscene Content on Social Media
GS2.explainer:
• Remarks made by Ranveer Allahbadia on the show 'India's Got Latent' have sparked nationwide outrage and intense debate.
• The controversy primarily revolves around the limits of free speech, particularly concerning obscene or inappropriate content on digital platforms.
• Legal experts and civil society groups are discussing the applicability and enforcement of laws pertaining to obscenity and content moderation on social media.
• The incident has reignited calls for clearer guidelines and stricter enforcement mechanisms for user-generated content.
• It highlights the ongoing challenge of balancing creative expression with societal norms and legal frameworks in the digital age.
prelimsPoints: Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
Article 19(2) outlines reasonable restrictions on free speech, including public order, decency, and morality.
Sections of the Information Technology Act, 2000, dealing with obscene content and intermediary liability.
Definition of 'obscenity' as interpreted by Indian courts (e.g., Ranjit Udeshi case).
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in defining and regulating obscene content in the context of evolving social media platforms and diverse user bases.
Critically analyze the current legal framework in India for content moderation and its effectiveness in balancing free speech with public decency.
Examine the role of social media intermediaries in controlling objectionable content and their responsibilities under Indian law.
Suggest policy recommendations for fostering responsible digital citizenship and creating a safer online environment while upholding fundamental rights.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized illustration of a speaker icon with blurred social media feeds in the background, representing online discourse.
GS2.trivia: The 'safe harbour' provisions under Section 79 of the IT Act protect intermediaries from liability for third-party content under certain conditions.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Freedom of Speech and Expression and its reasonable restrictions

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Leaders Pledge Enhanced Cooperation in Maritime Security and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key outcomes included pledges for deeper cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and disaster management in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both sides emphasized boosting bilateral trade and investment, with a focus on supply chain resilience and digital economy integration.
• Discussions also covered regional connectivity projects and strengthening people-to-people ties.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic alignment with the ASEAN bloc.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) members and its headquarters.
India's 'Act East Policy' (formerly 'Look East Policy') objectives.
Importance of the Indo-Pacific region for global trade and security.
Major initiatives under India-ASEAN cooperation (e.g., Delhi Dialogue, India-ASEAN Free Trade Area).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic imperatives driving India's enhanced engagement with ASEAN in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the significance of maritime security cooperation between India and ASEAN for regional stability and freedom of navigation.
Evaluate the potential for boosting trade and economic integration between India and the ASEAN bloc, identifying key opportunities and challenges.
Examine how India's 'Act East Policy' contributes to its broader foreign policy objectives and its role as a regional power.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states waving together, symbolizing partnership and cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1995, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: International Relations
prelimsSyllabus.topic: ASEAN and India's relations with it

GS2.headline: Parliament Approves 'Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2025' for Decriminalizing Minor Offences
GS2.explainer:
• Both Houses of Parliament passed the 'Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2025', a landmark legislation aimed at decriminalizing minor economic and procedural offences.
• The Bill proposes to amend various acts, replacing imprisonment with monetary penalties for minor violations, thereby reducing the burden on the judicial system.
• It seeks to promote ease of doing business and living by fostering trust-based governance and reducing legal complexities for citizens and businesses.
• The legislation is expected to significantly decongest courts and improve the efficiency of the justice delivery system.
• Industry bodies and legal experts have welcomed the move as a progressive step towards modernizing India's regulatory framework.
prelimsPoints: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2023 (original bill).
Concept of decriminalization and its benefits.
Impact of minor offences on the judicial system and ease of doing business.
Role of various ministries in identifying and amending laws for decriminalization.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2025 promotes 'ease of doing business' and 'ease of living' in India.
Analyze the potential impact of decriminalizing minor offences on the Indian judicial system and its efficiency.
Critically evaluate the balance struck by the Bill between deterrence and trust-based governance.
Examine the broader implications of such reforms for India's regulatory environment and its attractiveness for investment.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a balance scale with 'Justice' on one side and 'Ease of Business' on the other, symbolizing the bill's objectives.
GS2.trivia: The original Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2023, proposed to amend 183 provisions across 42 Central Acts.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Ease of Doing Business and Judicial Reforms

GS2.headline: UN Human Rights Council Discusses Global Refugee Crisis, India Reiterates Humanitarian Aid
GS2.explainer:
• The United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC) convened to address the escalating global refugee crisis and discuss international response strategies.
• Member states highlighted the urgent need for increased humanitarian aid, protection for displaced populations, and durable solutions for refugees.
• India reiterated its long-standing commitment to humanitarian principles, emphasizing its history of providing assistance to refugees and displaced persons.
• The discussions focused on shared responsibility, equitable burden-sharing, and addressing root causes of forced displacement.
• The UNHRC called for greater international cooperation and adherence to international refugee law.
gsSubTopics: Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: UN Human Rights Council (UNHRC) structure, mandate, and headquarters.
Difference between a refugee, an asylum seeker, and an internally displaced person.
India's stance on the 1951 Refugee Convention and its 1967 Protocol (India is not a signatory).
Key international organizations dealing with refugees (e.g., UNHCR).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical approach and humanitarian principles in dealing with refugee populations, despite not being a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention.
Analyze the challenges posed by the global refugee crisis for international peace, security, and human rights.
Critically evaluate the concept of 'shared responsibility' and 'burden-sharing' in addressing forced migration on a global scale.
Suggest policy measures for India to develop a comprehensive and consistent national refugee policy in line with international best practices and its humanitarian ethos.
GS2.imageDescription: A symbolic image of a group of people walking towards a distant horizon, representing displaced populations.
GS2.trivia: The principle of 'non-refoulement' prohibits countries from returning refugees to places where their lives or freedom would be threatened.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Review: Focus on Vocational Training Integration
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlights significant progress in integrating vocational education into mainstream schooling from an early age.
• Special emphasis was placed on skill development, industry linkages, and experiential learning across educational levels.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to quality vocational programs.
• The review underscores the government's commitment to making education holistic, flexible, and employment-oriented.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of the National Education Policy 2020.
Recommendations of NEP 2020 regarding vocational education integration.
Role of various bodies like National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC).
Concept of 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrating vocational training into mainstream education as envisioned by the National Education Policy 2020 for India's demographic dividend.
Analyze the challenges faced in the effective implementation of vocational education reforms under NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure and teacher training.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 aims to make education more holistic, flexible, and employment-oriented, and its potential impact on India's workforce.
Suggest strategies to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality vocational education across all regions and socio-economic strata.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in a vocational training workshop, symbolizing practical skill development.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 aims to achieve 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2035.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: National Education Policy and its impact

GS2.headline: E-Governance Push: New Portal Launched for Seamless Delivery of Public Services
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a comprehensive e-governance portal for streamlined public service delivery.
• The platform integrates various government services, enabling citizens to access them digitally from a single window, reducing red tape and improving efficiency.
• It aims to enhance transparency, accountability, and citizen-centric governance through digital transformation.
• Features include online applications, status tracking, digital document submission, and a unified grievance redressal mechanism.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards realizing the vision of 'Digital India' and improving citizen-government interaction.
gsSubTopics: Applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of the Digital India program.
Various e-governance initiatives in India (e.g., MyGov, DigiLocker, UMANG app).
Concept of 'Good Governance' and its components.
Role of Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of e-governance initiatives in promoting transparency, accountability, and citizen-centric service delivery in India.
Analyze the benefits and challenges associated with implementing a single-window e-governance portal for accessing diverse public services.
Evaluate how digital transformation in governance contributes to 'ease of living' and reduces corruption and red tape.
Suggest measures to ensure digital inclusion, address privacy concerns, and enhance the cybersecurity of such e-governance platforms.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized hand interacting with a holographic interface displaying various government service icons.
GS2.trivia: The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app provides access to over 1200 government services on mobile.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting, Focus on Global Economic Stability
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, focusing on global economic stability, sustainable finance, and debt vulnerabilities.
• Discussions addressed macroeconomic challenges, including inflation, supply chain disruptions, and climate finance for developing nations.
• India emphasized the need for coordinated global policy responses to foster inclusive and resilient economic growth.
• The meeting aimed to build consensus on strengthening multilateral development banks and reforming international financial architecture.
• Delegates underscored the importance of digital public infrastructure and financial inclusion for global economic recovery.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora
prelimsPoints: G20 members and its formation.
Objectives of the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
Concept of climate finance and its importance for developing countries.
Role of multilateral development banks (e.g., World Bank, IMF).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role and priorities in steering the G20 agenda, particularly concerning global economic stability, sustainable finance, and debt relief for vulnerable nations.
Analyze the challenges faced by the global economy, such as persistent inflation, geopolitical fragmentation, and climate change, and the need for coordinated international responses.
Evaluate the significance of reforming multilateral development banks and strengthening the international financial architecture for equitable global growth.
Examine the potential of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and financial inclusion in driving sustainable economic development and bridging global inequalities.
GS2.imageDescription: A conference table with flags of G20 nations, symbolizing international cooperation and economic discussions.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Optimism on Inflation and Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision reflects a cautious approach amidst global uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures, despite a positive growth outlook.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to remain vigilant on inflation, targeting the 4% medium-term goal.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Experts anticipate continued stability in lending rates, supporting economic recovery while managing price stability concerns.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member body responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Key objectives of monetary policy in India: price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target set by the government for the RBI (currently 4% with a band of +/- 2%).
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate amidst prevailing economic conditions, both domestic and global.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and fostering economic growth in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the imperative of supporting economic recovery.
Examine the implications of the MPC's stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting an upward trending arrow indicating growth, superimposed with the RBI logo and financial charts.
GS3.trivia: The first meeting of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was held in October 2016.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, a crucial step towards developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
• This technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making India a more competitive player in the global space market.
• The RLV program is part of ISRO's long-term vision to enhance space access and capabilities.
• Scientists lauded the precision of the autonomous navigation, guidance, and control systems during the complex maneuver.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program.
Concept of reusable launch vehicles and their economic advantages.
Difference between expendable and reusable launch vehicles.
Other countries/agencies developing RLV technology (e.g., SpaceX, Blue Origin).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of reusable launch vehicles compared to traditional expendable rockets.
Examine the technological challenges involved in designing and operating RLVs, including materials science, guidance systems, and thermal protection.
Evaluate how ISRO's RLV program aligns with India's broader ambitions in space exploration and commercial space services.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a controlled landing.
GS3.trivia: ISRO first conducted a successful RLV-TD atmospheric re-entry experiment in 2016.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Phase 2 of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Boosts Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and demand.
• This phase introduces enhanced financial incentives for both electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key objectives include reducing fossil fuel imports, creating new job opportunities, and promoting indigenous manufacturing capabilities.
• Stakeholders praised the government's commitment to accelerating the transition to a sustainable energy future.
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme
prelimsPoints: Definition of 'Green Hydrogen' (produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy).
Objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Difference between green, grey, and blue hydrogen.
Role of electrolysers in green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security, climate change commitments, and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentives and policy frameworks in promoting indigenous manufacturing and demand for green hydrogen.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen on various sectors of the Indian economy and its contribution to decarbonization efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a leaf merging with a hydrogen atom symbol, representing green hydrogen.
GS3.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

GS3.headline: India's Industrial Production Records Robust Growth in Q3, Manufacturing Sector Leads Recovery
GS3.explainer:
• India's industrial production registered robust growth in the third quarter of the fiscal year 2024-25, exceeding market expectations.
• The manufacturing sector emerged as the primary driver of this recovery, showing significant expansion across various sub-sectors.
• Data from the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation indicated positive trends in capital goods and consumer durables segments.
• This growth is attributed to strong domestic demand, government infrastructure spending, and improving global supply chains.
• Economists view this as a positive sign for overall economic recovery and job creation in the coming months.
prelimsPoints: Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is a composite indicator that measures the growth rate in different industry groups of the economy.
IIP is compiled and published by the Central Statistical Office (CSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
Eight core industries that have a significant impact on IIP.
Components of IIP: Manufacturing, Mining, and Electricity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the robust growth in India's industrial production, particularly the manufacturing sector, in the recent quarter.
Discuss the significance of industrial growth for overall economic recovery, job creation, and achieving India's development goals.
Examine the role of government policies, such as 'Make in India' and Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes, in boosting manufacturing output.
Evaluate the challenges that persist for the industrial sector, including global trade uncertainties and supply chain disruptions, and suggest measures to sustain growth.
GS3.imageDescription: A factory floor with machinery in operation, symbolizing industrial growth and productivity.
GS3.trivia: The base year for the Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is currently 2011-12.

GS3.headline: Border Infrastructure Development Along LAC Picks Up Pace Amidst Geopolitical Shifts
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly accelerated the development of critical border infrastructure along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.
• The focus is on improving road networks, bridges, tunnels, and forward airbases to enhance military mobility and logistical capabilities.
• This intensified development is part of India's strategic response to ongoing border tensions and aims to bolster national security.
• The projects are being executed by various agencies, including the Border Roads Organisation (BRO), employing advanced construction techniques.
• Experts view this as a crucial step to bridge the infrastructure gap with adversaries and ensure effective troop deployment and rapid response.
